name: E3NN Tensor Product Two-Qubit

pytorch.reason: all tensor ops, density matrices, von Neumann entropy

P1_FCTP_1o_x_1o_equivariance.test: FCTP(1o ⊗ 1o → 0e+1e+2e) equivariant under simultaneous SO(3) rotation
P1_FCTP_1o_x_1o_equivariance.note: simultaneous rotation of both Bloch vectors; output rotates by block-diagonal D. Tolerance 1e-4 reflects float32 precision for the full 9-dim output block.

P2_scalar_output_correlates_with_dot_pro.test: 0e scalar output of 1o ⊗ 1o correlates with Bloch vector dot product v1·v2
P2_scalar_output_correlates_with_dot_pro.note: CG(1,1→0) is the dot product; e3nn scalar channel must be proportional to it

P3_dot_product_vs_mutual_information.test: Bloch vector dot product v1·v2 correlates with mutual information I(A:B)
P3_dot_product_vs_mutual_information.note: for the cos(theta)|00>+sin(theta)|11> family, both MI and dot decrease monotonically

P4_scalar_is_rotation_invariant.test: FCTP scalar output is invariant under simultaneous rotation of both inputs
P4_scalar_is_rotation_invariant.note: 0e irrep is invariant under all SO(3) rotations; std across rotations should be ~0

N1_nn_bilinear_fails_equivariance.test: standard nn.Bilinear two-qubit map fails equivariance test
N1_nn_bilinear_fails_equivariance.note: nn.Bilinear has no rotational structure; e3nn FCTP is necessary for equivariance

N2_single_qubit_rotation_changes_scalar.test: rotating only ONE Bloch vector DOES change the scalar output (not jointly invariant)
N2_single_qubit_rotation_changes_scalar.note: scalar is invariant under SIMULTANEOUS rotation, but NOT single-qubit rotation. This confirms the scalar is v1·v2 (dot product), not |v1| or |v2|.

N3_bell_state_bloch_vectors_zero.test: Bell state Bloch vectors are both zero; scalar output should be 0
N3_bell_state_bloch_vectors_zero.note: maximally entangled state → maximally mixed marginals → zero Bloch vectors → zero dot product

B1_product_state_bloch_vectors_aligned.test: product state |00>: both Bloch vectors = (0,0,1), dot product = 1

B2_bell_state_bloch_zero_dot_zero.test: Bell state: both Bloch vectors = 0, dot product = 0

B3_interpolation_dot_and_mi_monotone.test: cos(theta)|00>+sin(theta)|11>: dot product decreases and MI increases with theta
B3_interpolation_dot_and_mi_monotone.note: dot product and MI are anti-correlated: more entanglement = smaller dot product

B4_identity_rotation_equivariance_zero.test: identity rotation: FCTP equivariance error is 0

key_finding: e3nn FullyConnectedTensorProduct(1o⊗1o→0e+1e+2e) is equivariant under simultaneous SO(3) rotation. The 0e scalar output is the dot product v1·v2, which anti-correlates with mutual information (Bell state: v1=v2=0, dot=0, MI=log2; product: dot=1, MI=0). e3nn naturally encodes two-qubit correlations via CG decomposition.
